zh.lifeSummary: 當William Carter於大約1420年出生在英國英格蘭賀特福爾金也斯 明日香時，他的父親John de Carter21歲，母親Alice Frazier of Doncaster21歲。他於1443年與Elizabeth Convert在英國貝德福爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1517年9月29日在英國英格蘭賀特福爾沃特福德享年98歲，埋葬在英國賀特福爾沃特福德。

nameMeanings.name: Carter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a transporter of goods, from Middle English carter(e) ‘carter’ (Anglo-Norman French car(e)tier, Old French charetier, medieval Latin carettarius, carettator). The Old French word coalesced with the earlier Middle English word cart(e) ‘cart’, which is from either Old Norse kartr or Old English cræt, both of which, like the Late Latin word, were probably derived from Celtic. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

variants.name: Charman
meanings: English (Sussex and Surrey): occupational name for a carter, carrier, or coachman, from Middle English (Old French) char, car ‘car, cart, carriage’ + Middle English man ‘man’. Compare Carter and Carman 2.

English (Sussex and Surrey): occupational name for someone who did odd jobs, from Middle English char(re), chere (Old English cier(r), cer(r), cear(r), cær(r)) ‘turn (of activity), small job, chore’ + man. Compare Carman 2.
In some cases probably also an Americanized form of Slovenian Čarman (see Carman 5).

variants.name: Chartier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Marcel, Adelard, Alcide, Emile, Gilles, Raoul, Rosaire, Alphonse.
French: shortened form of charretier, an occupational name for a carter, from an agent derivative of Old French charette ‘cart’.
French Canadian: altered form of English   Carter  , a cognate of 1 above. Compare   Sharkey   and   Shorkey  .

variants.name: Karter
meanings: German: occupational name for a carder (someone who prepares wool for spinning), from Middle High German karter. Compare Carter .

variants.name: McCarter
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   McArthur  .

variants.name: Carrier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Marcel, Emile, Lucien, Cecile, Alain, Gaetan, Gilles, Jacques, Laurent, Normand.

English and southern French: from Middle English, Old French car(r)ier (from Late Latin carrarius, a derivative of carrum ‘cart, wagon’, a word of Gaulish origin), an occupational name for a carter (see Carter ), in French also for a cartwright. Compare French Charrier .
French: occupational name for a stonemason or quarryman, carrier. Compare Coyer .

variants.name: Cartman
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): occupational name from Middle English cartman ‘man who operated a cart for the transport of goods’. Compare Carter .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When William Carter was born about 1420, in King's Langley, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, John de Carter, was 21 and his mother, Alice Frazier of Doncaster, was 21. He married Elizabeth Convert in 1443, in Bedfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died on 29 September 1517, in Watford, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 98, and was buried in Watford, Hertfordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Carter est né environ 1420, à King's Langley, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John de Carter, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Alice Frazier of Doncaster, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Convert en 1443, à Bedfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé le 29 septembre 1517, à Watford, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 98 ans, et a été enterré à Watford, Hertfordshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: William Carterが約1420年にイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアキング'ス ラングリーで生まれた時，父John de Carterは21歳，母Alice Frazier of Doncasterは21歳であった。1443年にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアでElizabeth Convertと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1517年9月29日にイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアワトフォードで98歳で死去し，イギリスハートフォードシュアイアワトフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Carter(이)가 대략 1420년 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 킨그'스 랭글리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de Carter(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Frazier of Doncaster(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1443년 영국 베드퍼드실르에서 Elizabeth Convert(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1517년 9월 29일 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 왓퍼드에서 98세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 허트퍼드실르 왓퍼드에 묻혔다.
